note: Lab-specific proposal content for the site Pitch section. Task examples reference heldout_task_candidates.json by id so SEC citations and rubric checks stay single-sourced.

labs.label: OpenAI proposal
labs.headline: TKO: it solved it, then lost it.
labs.thesis: TKO is the clearest OpenAI example: GPT-5.5 found the filing value, then replaced the requested basis with a plausible spot-price valuation. Sol repeated the same behavior with a different wrong number. Across the full run, basis selection accounts for 7 of GPT-5.5's 10 model-behavior misses and 14 of Sol's 17. The trace defines the intervention, and the complete stack shows it is not isolated.

lead_failure.title: TKO valuation-basis precedence
lead_failure.soundbite: Right retrieval, wrong basis, bad final decision.
lead_failure.why_it_matters: This is close to a reward spec. The model had the answer in the trace, so the product is not more documents. It is a basis-preservation check that rewards the filing basis over adjacent plausible math. Two generations make the same class of error on this task: GPT-5.5 substituted $3.92 billion, and GPT-5.6 Sol substituted $4.00 billion after searching the literal phrase 'aggregate value of $3.25 billion'.

backup_failure.title: General Mills guidance vintage
backup_failure.soundbite: It counted the beats, then used the wrong guidance vintage.
backup_failure.why_it_matters: This is a second convention miss: the model must separate original guidance from later revised guidance before scoring beat or miss.

pilot.ask: Run a two-week basis-selection and completeness pilot on a sealed finance-agent slice.
deliverables: A 200-task dataset with 120 train, 20 dev, and 60 sealed eval tasks.
Reference answers with atomic rubrics and expert-adjudication notes.
Labeled trajectories with tool calls, evidence, output, and cost.
A versioned verifier and reward specification for convention and coverage failures.
A decision report comparing base and tuned models on the sealed split.
pilot.success_gate: Fifteen-point absolute lift on the sealed slice, paired 95 percent lower bound above zero, improvement across multiple subtypes, no broader regression, and lower cost among correct completions.

training_design.title: How the basis-selection pilot works
training_design.intent: The target behavior is following the basis the prompt authorizes, not blindly copying filing values. Matched task pairs make legitimate recomputation part of the test.

steps.label: Observed override
steps.detail: TKO retrieves the filed consideration, then substitutes a spot-price valuation.

steps.label: Matched task pairs
steps.detail: One task requests the filed value. Its sibling explicitly requires recomputation.

steps.label: Candidate reward
steps.detail: Candidate rollouts are scored on basis, source, citation, calculation, and final value.

steps.label: Development choice
steps.detail: Compare reward setups and inspect exploit cases before choosing one for the sealed test.

steps.label: Sealed transfer
steps.detail: Test fresh issuers and filings. Filing precedence may improve only if recomputation does not regress.

illustrative_reward.label: One reward design to test
illustrative_reward.note: These weights are a starting point, not the answer. We compare this setup with strict pass-fail and other component mixes on development data. We choose one before the sealed test and do not change it after seeing those results.
illustrative_reward.cap: A wrong requested basis or unsupported contradiction caps total reward at 0.25. Both still fail the strict release gate.

exploit_tests: Copying the filing fails when the paired prompt explicitly requires recomputation.
Recomputing market value fails when the prompt asks for filing-stated consideration.
A correct number under the wrong basis receives no basis credit.
Extra citations earn no additional reward unless they support a required claim.
training_design.stop_rule: Do not expand if strict completion does not improve on the sealed slice, if legitimate recomputation regresses, or if exploit rate rises.

product.title: Basis-selection reward pack
product.what_we_build: Tasks where the filing contains an authoritative value and the trace also contains a tempting alternate basis. The verifier checks whether the model follows the basis requested by the prompt before computing. GPT-5.5 has 7 observed failures in this family, while Sol has 14 across the same full run.

product.title: Completeness and omission-control pack
product.what_we_build: Multi-part finance tasks with strict term coverage. The verifier rewards every requested field and penalizes confident partial answers that omit one deal term, security term, or causal window.
fixes: Security summaries that include exact offering size, restrictions, and redemption.
Merger summaries that include close date, consideration, fractional-share treatment, and delisting.
Causal-window answers that tie facts to the period the question asks about.

labs.label: Anthropic proposal
labs.headline: Delta: facts landed, framing missed.
labs.thesis: Delta is the clearest Anthropic example. Fable built a strong guidance breakdown but omitted the year-over-year EPS comparison. Across the full run, Fable has 7 basis-selection failures, 5 completeness failures, 1 retrieval miss, and 6 benchmark defects. Delta makes the completeness intervention concrete without implying that completeness is the largest family.

lead_failure.title: Delta guidance framing
lead_failure.why_it_matters: This is the kind of miss a finance customer feels. The answer sounds competent, but the decision comparison is incomplete because the required investor framing never gets stated.

backup_failure.title: Zillow revenue-trend direction
backup_failure.soundbite: It had the acquisition facts and rationale, then missed the trend direction.
backup_failure.why_it_matters: Zillow shows the same coverage-discipline problem without relying on a benchmark-quality story.

pilot.ask: Run a two-week framing, coverage, and retrieval pilot on Fable 5's highest observed miss families.
deliverables: A 200-task dataset with 120 train, 20 dev, and 60 sealed eval tasks.
Reference answers with investor-framing checks and omission-sensitive rubrics.
Labeled trajectories that mark where facts entered context and where framing was lost.
A verifier package for completeness, trend direction, evidence retrieval, and basis selection.
A decision report comparing base and tuned models on the sealed split.
pilot.success_gate: Fifteen-point absolute lift on the sealed slice, paired 95 percent lower bound above zero, improvement across coverage and convention subtypes, no retrieval regression, and lower cost among correct completions.

training_design.title: How the completeness pilot works
training_design.intent: The target behavior is completing the decision the analyst asked for. Longer answers do not earn credit unless the required field, period, and evidence line up.

steps.label: Observed omission
steps.detail: Delta finds the guidance facts but omits the required prior-year EPS comparison.

steps.label: Required decision
steps.detail: Experts name the required fields, time periods, accepted equivalents, and supporting evidence.

steps.label: Candidate reward
steps.detail: Candidate rollouts receive field-level and contradiction verdicts, with a cap for a missing critical field.

steps.label: Development checks
steps.detail: Test field echoing, citation dumping, schema stuffing, and verbose but incomplete answers.

steps.label: Sealed completion
steps.detail: Test fresh issuers and filings. Strict completion must improve without unsupported claims or answer bloat.

illustrative_reward.label: One reward design to test
illustrative_reward.note: These weights are a starting point, not the answer. We compare this setup with strict pass-fail and other component mixes on development data. We choose one before the sealed test and do not change it after seeing those results.

components.label: Critical fields
components.detail: A missing critical field caps the outcome.

illustrative_reward.cap: A missing decision-critical field or unsupported contradiction caps total reward at 0.25. Both still fail the strict release gate.

exploit_tests: Field echoing fails when a required value or comparison is absent.
Schema stuffing fails when one decision-critical field is wrong or omitted.
A concise complete answer beats a verbose answer that repeats the filing.
Citation dumping earns no added reward and unsupported claims count against the rollout.
training_design.stop_rule: Do not expand if strict completion is flat, unsupported claims or contradictions rise, or the model only becomes longer.

product.title: Investor-framing and completeness pack
product.what_we_build: Tasks where the model can find the facts but must preserve the exact investor comparison: year-over-year growth, trend direction, missing term, or causal window.
fixes: Guidance answers that explicitly state the comparison the investor needs.
Acquisition-strategy answers that tie actions to the requested revenue trend.
Security-term summaries that do not stop after the salient economics.

product.title: Basis-selection eval slice
product.what_we_build: Tasks where multiple conventions are plausible and the model must pick the one requested by the prompt or filing. The rubric scores the decision boundary before the arithmetic.

product.title: Multi-document retrieval coverage pack
product.what_we_build: Tasks that require more than endpoint retrieval. The verifier checks whether every period or filing needed by the question actually entered context before answer generation.
fixes: Multi-year trend retrieval with every period present.
Close-status retrieval that combines announcement and closing filings.
Penalty for answering from a single adjacent source when the prompt requires several.
